findings.evidence: Line 97 declares `lemma isCoercive_energyIntegrand_of_lower_bounds`; lines 99-101 include `hb : ‖b₀‖ ≤ beta` and `hc : mu ≤ c₀`. Adjacent APIs use `garding_energyIntegrand_self_of_bounds` at EnergyForm.lean:214 and `isCoercive_matrixBilinearForm_of_lower_bound` at UniformEllipticity.lean:230 only when the input really is a single lower bound.
findings.fix: Rename it to match the adjacent `..._of_bounds` convention, e.g. `isCoercive_energyIntegrand_of_bounds`, or explicitly mention both kinds of assumptions such as `..._of_lower_bound_of_drift_bound`; update the zero-drift wrapper references accordingly.
findings.issue: `isCoercive_energyIntegrand_of_lower_bounds` is misnamed: the theorem needs `hb : ‖b₀‖ ≤ beta`, an upper bound on the drift, not only lower bounds.

summary: One exported coercivity lemma has a misleading assumption suffix: it advertises lower bounds, but one of its essential hypotheses is an upper norm bound on the drift.
